title: History-mode prompt-box UX: inline Stop + prompt draft persistence
description: Two prompt-box UX gaps surfaced during MPI-112. (1) STOP BLOCKED: running an extend (or any prompt-box op) in the history workspace leaves the prompt-box Stop button disabled, forcing the user to go to the gallery, open the queue, and Stop from there. The history-mode prompt box should enable inline Stop for these ops. (2) PROMPT DRAFT NOT PERSISTED: typed prompt text is lost when navigating history<->gallery (and reportedly cleared by other actions). Expectation: a draft typed in either workspace survives nav back. Needs a state key holding the prompt draft (positive/negative) so PromptBox re-hydrates on mount. Both are prompt-box/state concerns, NOT the MPI-112 extend reuse-data fix. See brief.md.
